features.heading: Designed for the frontend
features.subheading: Client-side Japanese tokenization, without the usual server, dictionary, and privacy trade-offs.

size.title: Tiny footprint
size.desc: The WASM module and its built-in dictionary total under {size} gzipped — no multi-megabyte dictionary to fetch or host.

client.title: Client-side by design
client.desc: Runs entirely in the browser or your runtime. No tokenization server, no API round-trip, no CORS setup.

privacy.title: Text stays local
privacy.desc: Analysis happens on-device, so user input never leaves the browser and sensitive text stays out of your infrastructure.

unknown.title: Robust to unknown words
unknown.desc: Boundaries come from character patterns rather than a fixed dictionary, so brand names, slang, and technical terms stay intact.

realtime.title: Synchronous and instant
realtime.desc: Near-native WASM speed with a synchronous API. Tokenize on every keystroke without network latency.

typed.title: Typed, multi-runtime
typed.desc: A C++ core with first-class TypeScript types, plus Python and native C/C++ bindings for backend, batch, and embedded use.

comparison.title: Where Suzume fits
comparison.subtitle: TinySegmenter's light footprint, closer to MeCab's detail — Suzume sits between them.
comparison.guideTitle: There is more to the difference than this table
comparison.guideDescription: See concrete examples of token boundaries, dictionary dependence, and unknown-word handling.

useCase.title: Try it on your own text
useCase.subtitle: Every demo runs entirely in your browser — paste your own text and watch it tokenize.
useCase.tagDesc: Auto-generate hashtags from blog posts or product descriptions
useCase.excludeBasicTip: Filters out common verbs with hiragana-only lemma (する, いる, ある...)
useCase.baseDesc: Normalize verbs and adjectives to dictionary form for search indexing
useCase.analyzeDesc: Break Japanese text into linguistic tokens

typewriter.title: Tokenized as you read
typewriter.source: From "I Am a Cat" by Natsume Soseki
